subject: Cloud Computing
title: APIs: The Cloud's Interface
description: A short lesson to help you apply APIs: The Cloud's Interface.

hook.text: You never visit a cloud data center. You interact with the cloud entirely through APIs.

buildup.text: An API is a programmatic interface: send a request, get a response. Create a server in one call.

discovery.text: Every cloud service — storage, compute, AI — exposes an API. That's how automation became possible.

twist.text: API design matters enormously. A bad API makes a great service painful. A great API makes it addictive.

climax.text: AWS's success came partly from having clean, well-documented APIs before anyone else.

punchline.text: The cloud's front door is an API. There is no other door.

quiz.question: Why are APIs critical to cloud computing?
options: They allow programmatic access to all cloud services, enabling automation
APIs are physical connectors that plug into cloud servers
Cloud providers only communicate with customers via email
